note: Generated by scripts/gen-registry-export.mjs — do not hand-edit.

engines.name: Grok Imagine
engines.tagline: Native image + video inside the Grok harness — already in your SuperGrok plan.
engines.subscription: SuperGrok ($30/mo) — ~30–100 quality images/day on rolling windows
bestFor: Cinematic + photographic heroes generated from inside a Grok session
High volume on a flat monthly fee (no per-image bill)
Fast iteration when you are already working in Grok
engines.notWhen: Text-heavy/technical diagrams where labels must be legible — Grok Imagine fabricates text. Use Nano Banana Pro (Search-grounded) for those.
engines.auth: Logged-in Grok CLI/app. NB: the CLI may sit on a base-tier token showing "free credits %" — re-login with the main SuperGrok account for full quota.

cost.summary: Included in SuperGrok — no extra API cost
cost.note: Rolling windows throttle bursts (50 then wait 2–6h). Video costs ~5–10× an image in quota.

engines.whenWorthPaid: For programmatic batches beyond the daily quota, the Grok Imagine API (~$0.02/image) supplements it.

install.label: Already native in the Grok harness
install.detail: Generate from a Grok session directly. Capacity guide: ~/.grok/docs/visual-generation-capacity.md.

engines.name: Nano Banana Pro (Antigravity)
engines.tagline: Gemini 3 Pro Image built into Antigravity — free, in-editor, and Search-grounded.
engines.subscription: Antigravity free public preview (NB2 free to all); Google AI Pro/Ultra for higher limits
bestFor: Text-heavy + technical heroes, infographics, diagrams — Search grounding keeps labels legible & accurate
Real-time-data visuals (current pricing, dates, charts) via Google Search
No-extra-cost generation from inside Antigravity / a Gemini session
engines.notWhen: You are working in a non-Gemini harness with no key — Claude reaches the same model via the infogenius script path instead.
engines.auth: Logged-in Antigravity / Gemini account (no separate API key inside Antigravity)

cost.summary: Free in the Antigravity preview / included in Google AI subscription

engines.whenWorthPaid: Programmatic batches outside Antigravity → the Gemini API (per-image pricing) via the infogenius script.

install.label: Native in Antigravity
install.detail: Ask the Antigravity agent to generate — it invokes Nano Banana Pro in-conversation. NB2 is the free default.

engines.name: gpt-image-2 (Codex)
engines.tagline: OpenAI image gen built into Codex CLI via the $imagegen skill — on your ChatGPT plan.
engines.subscription: ChatGPT (Free/Go/Plus/Pro/Business) — counts toward Codex usage limits
bestFor: Quick visuals while already coding in Codex
Strong text-in-image rendering on a sub you already pay for
engines.notWhen: Large batches — image turns burn the Codex limit ~3–5× faster. Switch to a per-call API for volume.
engines.auth: Codex signed in with a ChatGPT plan. Setting OPENAI_API_KEY switches it to paid API pricing.

cost.summary: Included in the ChatGPT plan (no extra cost); ~3–5× faster limit burn per image turn

engines.whenWorthPaid: Batches of >250 images, or to avoid eating Codex coding quota → set OPENAI_API_KEY for gpt-image-2 API pricing.

install.label: Native in Codex CLI
install.detail: Built in since Apr 2026. Uses the ChatGPT plan by default; API key only for paid/volume.

engines.name: InfoGenius / Nano Banana
engines.tagline: Gemini-3 premium hero stills with Guardian AI routing — the in-house engine.
bestFor: Hero imagery at the top of a page
Book + album covers that live for years
Research-grounded infographics (the InfoGenius pipeline)
engines.notWhen: Bulk B-roll or 20-attempts-in-10-minutes ad variants — that volume burns time at hero quality.

cost.note: The durable path (scripts/lib/nb-image.mjs) needs no server and finds the key automatically.

install.label: Option A — durable library (no server, recommended)
install.detail: Already in-repo. Reads GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY from Arcanea/.env.local automatically. Most reliable across reinstalls.

install.label: Option B — the arcanea-infogenius MCP (when the binary is present)
install.detail: arcanea-infogenius-mcp v2.0.0 (Gemini 3 Pro + Guardian routing). Note: the binary is not in the current Arcanea tree — rebuild it there before registering, and pass GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY via env expansion, never a key pasted into a committed .mcp.json. The Option A library needs none of this.

engines.name: Higgsfield
engines.tagline: One OAuth endpoint fronting 30+ frontier models — Sora, Veo, Kling, Soul.
bestFor: Interactive cinematic video from inside the harness
Character-consistent B-roll via Soul ID training
Reaching Sora / Veo / Kling without juggling three API keys
engines.notWhen: High-volume programmatic batch — the subscription-credit model gets expensive, and MCP calls burn credits even in "unlimited" mode.
engines.auth: OAuth through your Higgsfield account (no API key to manage)

cost.note: MCP generations always consume credits, even when the web UI shows "unlimited".

install.label: Register the MCP server
install.command: claude mcp add --transport http higgsfield https://mcp.higgsfield.ai/mcp
install.detail: Already registered at project scope in this repo.

install.label: Authenticate
install.detail: Approve the project-scope server on next launch, then complete the OAuth browser login. Requires a paid Higgsfield plan — MCP calls fail on credits otherwise.

engines.name: fal.ai
engines.tagline: Pay-per-use aggregator — the economical lane for batch video and bulk B-roll.
bestFor: Lyric-video batches across the 12k catalog
Bulk B-roll where per-generation cost matters
Programmatic generation from scripts (one API, many models)
engines.notWhen: A single premium hero still — use InfoGenius. Interactive cinematic exploration — use Higgsfield.

cost.summary: 30–80% cheaper than subscription credits on video; ~$0.025–0.05/image

install.label: Get a key
install.detail: Create FAL_KEY at fal.ai/dashboard/keys and add it to .env.local.

install.label: Call via the AI SDK or a thin script wrapper
install.detail: Wrap in scripts/lib/fal-image.mjs mirroring nb-image.mjs so the gen router treats it as one more backend.

engines.name: GPT Image 2
engines.tagline: OpenAI image lane — a second opinion via the key you already have.
bestFor: A different aesthetic read when InfoGenius output feels off-lane
Strong text-in-image rendering
Teams already standardized on OpenRouter
engines.notWhen: Your default hero work — InfoGenius is the in-house premium tier and stays the default.
engines.auth: OpenRouter key (OPENROUTER_API_KEY — already on this machine)

install.label: Route through OpenRouter
install.detail: OPENROUTER_API_KEY + OPENROUTER_BASE_URL are already set (machine-global LLM policy). Add a scripts/lib/gpt-image.mjs wrapper that posts to the images endpoint.

engines.name: Sora · Veo · Kling (direct)
engines.tagline: The raw frontier video models, direct — cheapest per call, most plumbing.
bestFor: Squeezing per-call cost on one specific model at high volume
A production pipeline already standardized on one provider
engines.notWhen: You want one connector + Soul character consistency — Higgsfield fronts all three for far less setup.

cost.summary: Lowest per-generation, but three separate integrations + no Soul consistency

install.label: Only if you outgrow Higgsfield on one model
install.detail: Wire the single provider SDK you actually need. Most GenCreator users should reach these through Higgsfield, not directly.
